mainEntity.name: Can I sue my employer for negligence if I got sick from toxic chemicals?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually, no; workers\' comp is your only option against an employer. However, you CAN sue a "third party"—like a chemical manufacturer or a mask maker—if their defective product caused your illness, which allows for pain and suffering damages.
acceptedAnswer.description: In Utah and Idaho, workers\' comp is the "exclusive remedy," meaning you generally cannot sue your boss for a mistake or negligence that caused a disease. However, there are "third-party" exceptions. If your illness was caused by a defective machine made by another company, or a toxic chemical manufactured by a third party, you can sue that manufacturer for full damages (including pain and suffering). Also, if an employer "intentionally" harmed you (which is a very high bar), the rules may change. We investigate every claim to see if there is a third party we can hold accountable, as this often results in much higher compensation than workers\' comp alone.
